content:
Member-only story

Every Angular Project Starts the Same Way

3 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The first component is tiny.
@Component({...})
export class CustomerComponent {}It renders a page.
Everything feels simple.
Then the feature grows.
A button is added.
Validation appears.
API calls arrive.
Permissions are introduced.
Business rules slowly accumulate.
Nothing feels wrong.
Until one component quietly becomes the entire application.
Not a Member? Read for FREE here.
The Component Becomes the Business
Open many enterprise Angular projects and you’ll find components that:

• Fetch data

• Validate permissions

• Calculate discounts

• Format business values

• Call multiple APIs

• Manage state

• Handle workflows

• Track analytics
